skill.name: mcp-server-best-practices
skill.description: Guidance for designing, securing, testing, and operating production MCP servers with Rust-oriented examples.
skill.summary: Apply production patterns for secure, maintainable, and observable MCP servers.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported code execution, network, environment, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these in Markdown Rust examples rather than executable skill logic. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, hidden command execution, or malicious intent was found. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because users may still copy illustrative snippets without production hardening.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The backtick findings occur at Rust Markdown code fence boundaries and example sections. They are not Ruby shell execution and do not run commands from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each flagged location is part of a fenced Markdown code example. No executable script file or command runner is present in the scanned skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Rust Mock Method Flagged as With Statement
low_findings.description: The lines use the Rust mockall .with matcher inside unit test examples. This is not a JavaScript with statement and does not alter execution scope.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is a Rust test mock expectation chain. The detected token is a method name, not a deprecated language statement.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network and Environment Access Are Illustrative Examples
low_findings.description: The fetch, external API, request handler, and database URL references appear in architectural and performance examples. They do not contact remote services or read secrets during skill use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited lines are Rust documentation snippets inside SKILL.md. There is no runnable integration, endpoint, or environment variable read performed by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Textual Tokens
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary words and Rust identifiers such as description, design, and Deserialize. No weak hash or cipher implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines do not contain MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak primitives. The matches are scanner token collisions in prose and Rust type names.

low_findings.title: Low Risk: Example Snippets Require Production Hardening
low_findings.description: Some security examples are intentionally concise, including JWT validation and SQL injection contrast snippets. Users should adapt them with full issuer, audience, key rotation, and error handling controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is educational and not malicious, but concise examples can be copied without complete production controls. This is a content-quality security concern, not a marketplace compromise indicator.

content.value_statement: MCP server projects often fail when architecture, security, testing, and operations are added too late. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical review guidance for production-ready server design.
actual_capabilities: Reviews MCP server architecture using layered and hexagonal design patterns.
Recommends input validation, authorization, and parameterized database access practices.
Guides error handling, retries, timeouts, and structured service errors.
Suggests unit, integration, and container-backed testing approaches for server logic.
Covers monitoring, metrics, configuration loading, and operational checklists.
limitations: Examples are primarily Rust-oriented and assume familiarity with async server development.
It does not execute tests, inspect live infrastructure, or verify deployed MCP servers.
Security snippets are illustrative and need project-specific threat modeling.
It does not replace framework documentation or compliance review.

use_cases.title: Design a New MCP Server
use_cases.description: Plan service layers, ports, adapters, and domain boundaries before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Review Security Before Release
use_cases.description: Check validation, authorization, database access, and configuration patterns before publishing a server.

use_cases.title: Improve Operations Readiness
use_cases.description: Add testing, logging, metrics, and configuration practices to make an MCP server easier to maintain.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small MCP server design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my MCP server structure for clear layers, maintainable modules, and obvious missing pieces. Focus on practical changes first.

prompt_templates.title: Check Security Controls
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate security review before implementation or release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this MCP server plan for input validation, authentication, authorization, database safety, and secret handling. List risks and concrete fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Tests and Observability
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate readiness planning for a service that needs production support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a test and observability plan for this MCP server. Include unit tests, integration tests, logs, metrics, and failure cases.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced end-to-end architecture and operations review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a production readiness audit for this MCP server. Assess architecture, security, performance, testing, configuration, deployment, and maintenance risks.

output_examples.input: Review a planned user management MCP server.
output: Architecture review with suggested service, domain, and repository boundaries.
Security checklist covering validation, roles, token handling, and database access.
Testing plan with unit tests, integration tests, and failure scenarios.

output_examples.input: Improve a server that has slow external API calls.
output: Timeout and retry recommendations for external dependencies.
Caching guidance with expiration and capacity considerations.
Metrics to track latency, errors, and request volume.

output_examples.input: Prepare an MCP server for production deployment.
output: Release checklist for configuration, logging, metrics, and health checks.
Risk notes for secrets, migrations, and dependency auditing.
Maintenance recommendations for documentation and API versioning.

best_practices: Start with clear service boundaries before adding adapters, databases, or external APIs.
Treat validation, authentication, authorization, and secret handling as core design requirements.
Pair every production feature with tests, logs, metrics, and documented failure behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not copy concise examples into production without project-specific security hardening.
Do not mix transport handling, business rules, and infrastructure code in one module.
Do not rely on logs alone when metrics, health checks, and tests are needed.

faq.question: What type of MCP server does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps with production-oriented MCP servers, especially Rust services that need clean architecture and operational discipline.

faq.question: Can it generate complete server code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation and review code, but the skill itself is guidance rather than a complete framework.

faq.question: Does it perform a live security scan?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and checklists. Use dedicated scanners and manual review for final security validation.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompts and guidance are suitable for Claude, Claude Code, and Codex workflows.

faq.question: Are the examples production ready as written?
faq.answer: No. They show patterns and need adaptation for your framework, threat model, dependencies, and deployment environment.

faq.question: What should I provide when using this skill?
faq.answer: Provide your server goals, architecture notes, code snippets, data flows, authentication model, and deployment constraints.
